title: FeeManager updateState front-run vulnerability
reason: `updateState` writes state keyed by `msg.sender` vault; arbitrary callers cannot alter another vault’s fee state, so the claimed front-run/censorship path is unsupported.

title: RiskManager asset disallow leaves balance accounting inconsistent
ShareModule claimableSharesOf unbounded loop vulnerability
reason: Substantially overlaps existing F-005; no materially new exploit path beyond already tracked transfer-time auto-claim gas liveness risk.

title: Oracle removeSupportedAssets leaves queue states in inconsistent state
Signature queues bypass queueLimit cap
reason: Unsubstantiated: standalone/deployed signature queues are not recognized by vault queue sets, and critical calls (`mint`/`burn`/risk updates/hooks) are blocked by queue membership checks.

title: FeeManager lacks proper initialization guard
reason: Speculative proxy-misconfiguration scenario; no concrete in-code reinitialization path in scoped implementation.

title: Verifier CUSTOM_VERIFIER verificationData parsing off-by-one
reason: Not a distinct issue from existing F-017; the actionable root cause is wire-format mismatch, not an independent off-by-one memory safety bug.

title: BasicRedeemHook partial fill causes inconsistent state
reason: Unsupported: if hook fails to source enough assets, downstream vault->queue transfer reverts and state changes in `handleBatches` do not persist.

title: LidoDepositHook silent failure on wrap/withdraw
reason: Unsupported: external calls (`wrap`, `withdraw`) are not swallowed; failures revert transaction.

title: TokenizedShareManager auto-claim on every transfer creates griefing surface
Consensus threshold can be zero through threshold validation gap
reason: Unsupported: `_setThreshold` explicitly reverts on `threshold_ == 0` and on values above signer count.

title: Factory.create uses predictable salt enabling front-running
reason: No concrete protocol-harmful exploit shown; public creation with predictable salt does not by itself enable theft/lockup or privileged takeover in this implementation.

title: VaultConfigurator setVault called in non-atomic sequence enabling hijack
reason: Duplicate of existing F-006 (non-atomic `setVault` hijack risk).
